name: Upgrade shell-quote to >=1.8.4 — newline command injection (CVE-2026-9277)
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L13-04] shell-quote@1.8.3 (pulled via @expo/cli→react-native CLI chain) does not escape newlines, allowing shell command injection in dev tooling (CVE-2026-9277), fixed >=1.8.4. Root cause: pnpm-lock resolves shell-quote to 1.8.3 with no override pinning it to the safe version. Fix: add a pnpm override `"shell-quote": ">=1.8.4"` in the root package.json overrides block.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: shell-quote@1.8.3 resolved transitively via @expo/cli; CVE-2026-9277 allows newline injection in shell argument quoting, enabling command injection in dev tooling. Behavioral variants: (a) Expo CLI invoked with crafted input (e.g. project name, file path, env var containing newline) — injection possible; (b) normal Expo CLI invocation with clean inputs — no exploit path. Single CVE surface in the dev/CI toolchain only (not production runtime). Regression verification: after adding pnpm override `"shell-quote": ">=1.8.4"` and running `pnpm install`, confirm `pnpm ls shell-quote` reports no version below 1.8.4. Run the mobile app's typecheck and test suite (`pnpm exec nx run mobile:typecheck && pnpm exec nx lint mobile`) — all must stay green. Fix is done when: (1) pnpm-lock.yaml records shell-quote >=1.8.4 only, (2) `pnpm audit` no longer flags CVE-2026-9277, (3) mobile toolchain checks stay green.
riskImpact: shell-quote@1.8.3 (CVE-2026-9277) fails to escape newlines, allowing shell command injection in dev tooling pulled via @expo/cli. If a malicious string is passed through the dev toolchain (e.g., a crafted project name or environment variable), it can escape the shell argument and execute arbitrary commands in the developer's or CI environment. Blast radius: development and CI environments running Expo CLI; not the production runtime.
